subject: Computer Networking
title: CSS — Styling the Web
description: A fast breakdown of CSS — Styling the Web for builders.

hook.text: HTML builds the skeleton. CSS paints it. Every color, font, and layout on the web comes from CSS.

buildup.text: Selectors target HTML elements. Properties set values: color, margin, font-size, display, and hundreds more.

discovery.text: The cascade resolves conflicts: inline beats internal, internal beats external. Specificity is the tiebreaker.

twist.text: Responsive design uses media queries. One stylesheet adapts the layout to phones, tablets, and desktops.

climax.text: Flexbox and Grid transformed layout. Before them, developers hacked layouts with floats and tables.

punchline.text: CSS makes the web beautiful. Separation of structure and style.

quiz.question: What CSS feature enables layouts that adapt to different screen sizes?
